steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Left side of the blank: “The number of jams available for tasting varied” is an independent clause. Right side: “some shoppers had twenty-four different options, others only six” begins with an independent clause and includes an elliptical clause where the verb is omitted.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list or elaboration after an independent clause. Here, it sets off “some shoppers had twenty-four different options, others only six” as the explanation of the variation.

  • B creates a comma splice
  • C misplaces a comma before the subordinating conjunction “while”
  • D uses a subordinating conjunction to form a dependent clause
, which is grammatically correct but not the standard punctuation for introducing an explanation.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
